title: Existence and uniqueness of fractal-fractional equations generated by a new fractal-fractional operator utilizing the advanced gamma function
author: Alazman, Ibrahim

description: The particular circumstances of the equations and the corresponding theorems determine the existence and uniqueness of solutions to differential equations. The standards for determining these attributes may vary throughout differential equation categories. In order to analyze and solve differential equations, the use of theorems such as the Picard-Lindelof theorem and uniqueness theorem is essential. In this section, we consider the following abstract fractal-fractional differential equation(1)bCΔtκ,νυ(t)=V(t,υ(t));or(2)bRLΔtκ,νυ(t)=V(t,υ(t)),where υ(0)=υ0 and V:[0,℘]×R→R is continuous and differentiable. Consider the Banach space B of all continuous functions over the interval J=[0,℘],℘<∞ subjecting to the sup norm. Next result shows the existence of nonzero solution for Eq. (1). Define the following operator: Q:S→S, whereS≔{υ∈B:∥υ∥≤2|υ0|1−Lν(1b)(κ/b+1)/b−1(b2℘b+κb2b+κ)},t∈[0,℘],℘<∞ andLν(1b)(κ/b+1)/b−1(b2℘b+κb2b+κ)<1 byQ(υ)(t)=υ0+ν∫0tV(τ,υ(τ))[Πκ]b(−λ(t−τ)κbdτ.Then we have|Q(υ)(t)|=|υ0+ν∫0tV(τ,υ(τ))[Πκ]b(−λ(t−τ)κbdτ|≤|υ0|+ν|∫0tV(τ,υ(τ))[Πκ]b(−λ(t−τ)κbdτ|≤|υ0|+ν∫0t|V(τ,υ(τ))||[Πκ]b(−λ(t−τ)κb|dτ≤|υ0|+νL∥υ∥∫0t|((t−τ)b)(κ/b+1)/b−1Ξ(1+κ/bb,1+κ/bb)(−λ(t−τ)(1+κ/b)/bb(1+κ/b)/b)|dτ≤|υ0|+νL∥υ∥(1b)(κ/b+1)/b−1×∫0t|(t−τ))(κ/b+1)/b−1Ξ(1+κ/bb,1+κ/bb)(−λ(t−τ)(1+κ/b)/bb(1+κ/b)/b)|dτ≤|υ0|+νL∥υ∥(1b)(κ/b+1)/b−1∫0t|(t−τ))(κ/b+1)/b−1|dτ≤|υ0|+νL∥υ∥(1b)(κ/b+1)/b−1(b2℘b+κb2b+κ)Thus, we obtain|Q(υ)(t)|≤|υ0|1−Lν(1b)(κ/b+1)/b−1(b2℘b+κb2b+κ),υ0≠0. By taking the supremum of the above inequality, we have Q∈S (boundedness of Q). We proceed to show that Q is continuous in S. Since V is continuous in J then it is uniformly continuous satisfying∥V(t,υ(t))−V(t,ψ(t))∥≤ϵν(1b)(κ/b+1)/b−1b2℘b+κb2b+κ.
